---
title: "Broomsedge Bluestem (Anatherum virginicum)"
slug: "anatherum-virginicum"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180780548/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Broomsedge Bluestem"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Poaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Poaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180780548/medium.jpg" alt="Broomsedge Bluestem" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/m_fab">
m_fab</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/107481301">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Poaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=1617172)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common to Florida and found in dry, open habitats.

## Natural history notes

A perennial grass characterized by its upright, clumping growth and distinctive broom-like seed heads that turn reddish-brown in fall. In Florida, it is commonly found in dry, open habitats such as fields, flatwoods, and roadsides. It grows primarily during the warmer months and is often an early colonizer of disturbed areas.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/1617172-Anatherum-virginicum)
-   [Local profile](https://www.fnps.org/plant/andropogon-virginicus#gsc.tab=0)
